notes: The game starts out of the box, and i have constant 60FPS. I was able to play the first 5 hours without any issues. Now, i have several freezes and crashes to desktop every 5-10 Minutes. I can't say is this game specific, or is there something wrong with Steam Proton? Btw. The game runs fine on Windows10.
title: Prey

notes: Works great without any tweaks. Framerate is noticeably lower than on Windows, but I haven't noticed any other issues.
title: Prey

notes: Singleplayer works perfectly, everything is good out-of-the-box except some crashes during playing. These crashes are easily fixed by increasing file descriptor limit per process and so the game does not crash anymore, everything works flawlessly.
title: Prey

notes: Works out of the box fine in 4k with DXVK. On XWayland the window mode with boarders is required to see the Vulkan output (tested with Plasma).
title: Prey

notes: Crashes to desktop and freezes are gone, since i modified necessary files for Steam Proton to increase file descriptor limit per process. This "tweak" is adressed closer to Steam Proton/Wine, than to the game itself. For those who have no idea what i am talking about: The buzzwords are "hard nofile 1048576" and "DefaultLimitNOFILE=1048576"
title: Prey

notes: I have done a complete playthrough of the main game without issue, haven't tried the dlc yet.

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box. Always 60+ fps on Very High graphics preset on my RX 480. == Keep in mind that random stuttering is a bug in the game itself, usually happening after changing graphics settings. So if you need to change your graphics settings: Save, change the settings, close out of the whole game and load back in. That should prevent random stuttering. ==

notes:
Overall, with no tweaks ran flawlessly except one issue -- Whenever a phantom did its golden orb chargeup attack, the game would lockup.

"PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 DefaultLimitNOFILE=1048576 DXVK_HUD=1 %command%" as others recommended solved the issue, after that game ran perfectly through and through for almost an entire playthrough. The only other issue i had is using the exit to desktop feature also locked up the game.

notes: My experience with this was good; though I did have to move it to an internal drive. Likely something game related; but compared to internal USB 3.0 just leads to foot-dragging load times. It also seemed to reduce stutter somewhat at higher graphical settings, can't say why. I went with gold instead of platinum, as there are a few far-from-game-breaking issues; when I change display settings, as an example, the game will sometimes freeze up entirely. This does not seem to happen during regular play.

notes: If anyone encounters issues where the game crashes immediately after first loading screen... I struggled immensely with the same issue. After extensive investigation the bug seems to be on Windows too and is something to do with the game being incompatible with programs using some other drivers (the Windows example was due some AirDroid equivalent program running on their PC), I believe mine was caused by an Anbox installation. Regardless, after testing the install on a LiveUSB and not encountering the issue I reinstalled Manjaro and it worked out of the box. Frames per second were slightly lower than expected despite trying all tweaks mentioned here on ProtonDB, but were generally playable 50-60fps regardless of settings @1440p (60fps stable with Dynamic Resolution enabled). Encountered some audio glitching (also apparent on some Windows installs) for example hearing Operators where they were not visible. Once restarted game to have NPC spawn where they were not before, and had one minor side quest fail to initiate after receiving quest dialogue. Completely playable and finished the game.
title: Prey

notes:
Esync may not do anything. I feel like there's potentially some misinformation here. Ran into random times when the game would freeze solved for me ONLY by low graphical settings in the game. From what I can see, the game has problems that occur with certain hardware - if not all - on higher settings from one or more graphical features. People with Windows also have this happen and its not Proton only. So you may need to play with better settings and deal with random freezes as far as I can tell. Spent 10 hours without a single freeze on low.

Also, there are possible mouse issues which is a Proton problem.
title: Prey
